chapter_title.ar: باب ضَمَانِ مَا يُصِيبُ الدَّوَابُّ وَ مَا لَا ضَمَانَ فِيهِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 42 – Responsibility of what hits the animals and what is not a responsibility regarding it from that

text: امْرَأَةً نَذَرَتْ أَنْ تُقَادَ مَزْمُومَةً فَدَفَعَهَا بَعِيرٌ فَخَرَمَ أَنْفَهَا فَأَتَتْ أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) تُخَاصِمُ صَاحِبَ الْبَعِيرِ فَأَبْطَلَهُ وَ قَالَ إِنَّمَا نَذَرْتِ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكِ ذَلِكِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ismail Bin Marrar, from Yunus, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> that a woman had vowed to be le bridled, so she was pushed by a camel and her nose was pierced. So she came over to Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> to dispute against the owner of the camel. But, he<sup>asws</sup> invalidated it (her claim) and said: ‘But rather, she had vowed (to be bridles), and that is not upon you (the camel owner)’.
